title: These 3 Stocks Lowered Their Share Counts Drastically in Q1
author: Leo Miller
description: Pinterest, Southwest Airlines, and United Therapeutics executed aggressive share buyback programs in Q1 2026, reducing their outstanding share counts by 16%, 5%, and 3.2% respectively. Pinterest spent $2 billion on repurchases despite being down 15% YTD, while Southwest spent $1.25 billion amid fuel cost headwinds. United Therapeutics utilized a $1.5 billion accelerated share repurchase program following positive clinical trial results for its Tyvaso treatment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite being down 15% YTD, Pinterest beat earnings expectations, achieved 18% YoY sales growth exceeding $1 billion, and executed an aggressive $2 billion buyback reducing share count by 16% with $2 billion remaining capacity. Strong operational performance and management confidence demonstrated through substantial capital allocation.

Mixed signals: company improved operating margins by 810 basis points and executed $1.25 billion buyback reducing share count by 5%, but faces significant headwinds from elevated jet fuel prices (22-cent EPS impact). Stock flat YTD despite operational improvements.

Stock up 10% YTD with strong momentum including 12% single-day gain after positive Tyvaso IPF trial results. Company executed $1.5 billion accelerated share repurchase program, reducing share count by 3.2%, with management expressing confidence in valuation disconnect. Tyvaso has potential $5-10 billion IPF market opportunity.

title: The Surprising Reason Airline Stocks Are Soaring
author: Lee Samaha
description: Airline stocks Delta, United, and Southwest have outperformed the market despite soaring jet fuel costs from Middle East conflict. Airlines have successfully offset higher fuel expenses by raising ticket prices due to strong consumer demand. Delta and United are positioned as winners due to diversified revenue streams and pricing power, while earnings expectations for 2026 have been lowered but remain stable for 2027.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Delta has demonstrated strong pricing power to offset fuel costs, outperformed S&P 500 with 17.4% gain, and is highlighted as a top stock to buy for 2026 due to diversified revenue streams and ability to maintain margins despite geopolitical headwinds.

United has successfully raised prices to cover fuel increases amid strong demand and is identified as a likely winner from capacity constraints. CEO confirmed strong demand persistence, and the company maintains stable 2027 earnings expectations.

While Southwest has benefited from strong consumer demand and pricing increases, it lacks the diversified revenue streams of Delta and United, making it less resilient to industry cyclicality. CEO expressed bullish sentiment but the company is less prominently featured as a long-term winner.

title: Here's Why Alaska Air Shares Popped Higher This Week
author: Lee Samaha
description: Alaska Air Group shares rose 12.7% this week as airline stocks climbed amid rising jet fuel costs. Southwest Airlines CEO's comments about successfully implementing seven consecutive fare increases without demand drops suggest airlines can offset fuel cost increases with higher prices. This positive trend could help Alaska Air recover from significant fuel cost headwinds that were expected to impact earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rose 12.7% this week. Management commentary from competitor Southwest suggests airlines can successfully pass fuel cost increases to customers through higher fares without losing demand, which could help Alaska Air offset its projected $3+ EPS impact from fuel costs in Q2.

CEO's presentation revealed seven consecutive successful fare increases with no demand drop-off and growing confidence in covering fuel increases with revenue increases, indicating strong pricing power and demand resilience in the airline sector.
Previously reported strong demand continuing from Q1 into Q2 despite rising fuel costs, demonstrating robust end-demand and pricing power in the airline industry.

title: SOUTHWEST AIRLINES DECLARES 189th QUARTERLY DIVIDEND
author: Prnewswire
description: Southwest Airlines (NYSE: LUV) Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share for shareholders of record as of June 11, 2026, with payment scheduled for July 2, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of the 189th consecutive quarterly dividend demonstrates consistent shareholder returns and financial stability. Regular dividend payments indicate the company's confidence in its cash flow generation and commitment to returning capital to shareholders, which is generally viewed positively by investors.

title: Prediction: These 2 Airline Stocks Will Rebound Before Year's End
author: Thomas Niel
description: Despite geopolitical tensions in the Middle East driving up oil prices and pressuring the airline industry, Delta Airlines and Southwest Airlines are positioned to rebound by year-end. Delta's strong premium ticket sales and Q4 2025 earnings growth provide resilience, while Southwest stands to benefit from Spirit Airlines' closure increasing low-cost carrier market share and potential fuel cost relief.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q4 2025 earnings growth of 45% year-over-year, sustained premium ticket sales momentum (14% growth in Q1 2026), and analyst expectations of only 5.3% earnings decline despite fuel cost headwinds position the company well for recovery by year-end.

Positioned to gain market share from Spirit Airlines' closure, potential for fuel cost relief from geopolitical resolution, and reasonable valuation at 10.7x forward earnings. Stock pullback from mid-$50s to under $40 creates upside potential if confidence improves.
